title: Proof Stacking

content.question: Do you have proof from all four angles, social (reviews), product (works), economic (ROI), authority (press)? What's missing?
content.equation: PSS = (Social + Product + Economic + Authority) × Sequence Fit ÷ 4
sop: Audit your current proof assets and sort them into four buckets: social, product, economic, and authority.
Identify the bucket that's empty or weakest and create one new proof asset for it this week.
Rate Social, Product, Economic, and Authority proof 1-10 each, and rate Sequence Fit 1-10 based on whether the right proof appears at the right funnel moment (see calculation, page 6)
Match proof type to buyer type, skeptical analytical buyers get economic and authority proof, social buyers get reviews and testimonials.
Re-audit monthly and replace your weakest proof asset with a stronger one as it becomes available.
content.explainer: Some buyers trust testimonials; others trust data. Stack all four so every buyer finds their proof.
questions: Do you have proof from all four angles, social (reviews), product (works), economic (ROI), authority (press)? What's missing?
Which of the four proof types do you have zero of right now?
Are you showing your strongest proof type to the buyer who actually cares about it, or just showing it to everyone?

definitions.def: Return on Investment — the profit or value you get back relative to what you spent. High ROI means each dollar spent produces a lot in return.

content.equation_example: Rate each proof 1-10: Social 8, Product 7, Economic 6, Authority 5; Sequence Fit 8. PSS = (8 + 7 + 6 + 5) × 8 ÷ 4 = 52. Higher means every buyer type finds proof that speaks to them, in the right order; lower means you're relying on one proof type and hoping it works for everyone.
content.closing_statement: Different buyers trust different kinds of proof.

caption:
Principle Nº 10: Proof Stacking

Multiple proof types compound trust. Some buyers trust testimonials; others trust data.

Ask yourself: Do you have proof from all four angles, social (reviews), product (works), economic (ROI), authority (press)? What's missing?
